channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: As Supreme Court expands Trump's immigration power, experts warn of steeper U.S. population decline
item.description: The U.S. population was already aging and tilting toward decline. After the Supreme Court confirmed Trump's power to deport hundreds of thousands of foreign migrants, population decline could accelerate.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. population was already aging and tilting toward decline. After the Supreme Court confirmed Trump's power to deport hundreds of thousands of foreign migrants, population decline could accelerate.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Inside a secretive Ukrainian team launching deep drone strikes at Russia
item.description: Ukraine's long-range drones are striking deep inside Russia, up to 1,200 miles away, hitting oil refineries and depots. NPR recently spent time with one of the Ukrainian strike teams launching drones at Russian targets.
item.content:encoded:
Ukraine's long-range drones are striking deep inside Russia, up to 1,200 miles away, hitting oil refineries and depots. NPR recently spent time with one of the Ukrainian strike teams launching drones at Russian targets.

(Image credit: Serhii Korovvayny for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: What would George Washington say? It's a busy year for people who portray him
item.description: America's 250th birthday has brought more events and new crowds for Revolutionary reenactors and interpreters. They say Washington's life holds important lessons for our current political divide.
item.content:encoded:
America's 250th birthday has brought more events and new crowds for Revolutionary reenactors and interpreters. They say Washington's life holds important lessons for our current political divide.

(Image credit: Michelle Gustafson for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: 4 ways to get creative with your leftovers (and save money on food)
item.description: Don't let your leftovers go to waste. Cookbook authors share clever storage techniques — like an "Eat Me First" box in your fridge — and cooking tricks to help you make the most of your food scraps.
item.content:encoded:
Don't let your leftovers go to waste. Cookbook authors share clever storage techniques — like an "Eat Me First" box in your fridge — and cooking tricks to help you make the most of your food scraps.

(Image credit: Pulse/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Emily Siner

item.title: Venezuelans in Colombia scramble to send aid as earthquakes death toll increases
item.description: Aid efforts are intensifying after twin earthquakes killed nearly 1,000 in Venezuela, with international teams arriving but a slow government response hampering relief on the ground.
item.content:encoded:
Aid efforts are intensifying after twin earthquakes killed nearly 1,000 in Venezuela, with international teams arriving but a slow government response hampering relief on the ground.

(Image credit: Esteban Vega La-Rotta)
item.dc:creator: Manuel Rueda

item.title: There's a beef about beef at the World Cup, as Argentina fans pour into Texas
item.description: It's about who produces the best, most succulent steaks, and how to prepare the meat. Argentina and Texas are two of the top cattle-raising areas of the world, where steak is deeply ingrained in diet and culture.
item.content:encoded:
It's about who produces the best, most succulent steaks, and how to prepare the meat. Argentina and Texas are two of the top cattle-raising areas of the world, where steak is deeply ingrained in diet and culture.

(Image credit: Julio Cortez)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. strikes Iran in response to a drone attack on a ship
item.description: The U.S. struck Iran on Friday in response to a drone attack a day earlier on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz. It's the most significant test yet to an interim understanding reached a week ago by the two countries.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. struck Iran on Friday in response to a drone attack a day earlier on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz. It's the most significant test yet to an interim understanding reached a week ago by the two countries.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Pete Buttigieg and his kids subject to CPS, police investigation after false report
item.description: Buttigieg, a potential 2028 Democratic presidential contender, shared his family's experience when police and Child Protective Services came to his home investigating an anonymous tip that was false.
item.content:encoded:
Buttigieg, a potential 2028 Democratic presidential contender, shared his family's experience when police and Child Protective Services came to his home investigating an anonymous tip that was false.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: 5 million have dropped ACA insurance after Trump and the GOP let prices skyrocket
item.description: The number of people who signed up for a health plan and then didn't pay their premium fell much more sharply than it has in years past. Trump officials blame fraud. Health policy experts blame costs.
item.content:encoded:
The number of people who signed up for a health plan and then didn't pay their premium fell much more sharply than it has in years past. Trump officials blame fraud. Health policy experts blame costs.

(Image credit: Patrick Sison)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: A day after Alito's testy response to Sotomayor's dissent, court says it was a 'misunderstanding'
item.description: During Supreme Court opinions Thursday, Justice Samuel Alito, who wrote the majority opinion in an asylum case, appeared to rebut Justice Sonia Sotomayor, who wrote the dissent. The move was highly unusual — and on Friday there was a coda.
item.content:encoded:
During Supreme Court opinions Thursday, Justice Samuel Alito, who wrote the majority opinion in an asylum case, appeared to rebut Justice Sonia Sotomayor, who wrote the dissent. The move was highly unusual — and on Friday there was a coda.

(Image credit: Alex Wong)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg
